Article.ArticleTitle: Characterization of quadriceps muscle injuries in National Basketball Association athletes and effects on player performance following injury.

AffiliationInfo.Affiliation: The University of Arizona College of Medicine - Phoenix, 475 N 5th St, Phoenix, AZ, 85004, USA. rpparmar@arizona.edu.
Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, Mayo Clinic, 5777 E Mayo Blvd, Phoenix, AZ, 85054, USA.
Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, Harbor-UCLA Medical Center, 1000 W Carson St, Torrance, CA, 90502, USA.
Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, Mayo Clinic, 5777 E Mayo Blvd, Phoenix, AZ, 85054, USA.
Department of Orthopaedic Surgery, Mayo Clinic, 5777 E Mayo Blvd, Phoenix, AZ, 85054, USA.

MedlineCitation.CoiStatement: Declarations. Ethics approval and consent to participate: This study was submitted to, and approved by, the Mayo Clinic Institutional Review Board (IRB). Specifically, the outcome of this IRB submission was that the requirement for IRB approval was waived and the need for consent to participate was deemed exempt after complete review by the Mayo Clinic IRB (#21-009369), given the use of a player records from a publicly-available database. Consent for publication: Not applicable. Competing interests: We would like to disclose the following conflicts of interests: Anikar Chhabra: Consultant for Arthrex and Zimmer Biomet; Sailesh Tummala: ImpactOrtho: support for education, Stryker Corp: hospitality payments. All other authors have no conflicts of interests to disclose.
